extraction.one_line_summary: YATES Simon won from 40-rider breakaway after attacking on Peyresourde, using track-style positioning in final kilometer to hold off BILBAO in narrow finish straight.
tactical_patterns: YATES Simon deliberately lost time earlier in Tour to help teammate Adam, positioning himself for stage hunting opportunities today.
40-rider breakaway succeeded because GC contenders refused to chase before time trial next day, conserving energy for Tourmalet stage.
YATES used track sprint tactics in final kilometer, forcing BILBAO to barriers limiting his sprint angle to one direction only.
WELLENS Tim hunting every KOM point rather than just jersey lead, signaling all-in strategy for polka dots during Pyrenean stages.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: Won stage using track positioning skills in narrow finish, showed tactical maturity by sacrificing GC early for stage opportunities
rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: positive: First Tour bridged to leaders solo on climb, nearly won sprint despite being isolated without teammates
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: neutral: Taking single KOM points on minor climbs reveals desperate point accumulation strategy, suggesting weakness on bigger mountains ahead
rider_signals.MULBERGER Gregor: negative: Despite two Giro stage wins, completely ineffective in 3-up sprint tactics, allowed YATES to dictate positioning
